Destination - Better protect the EU and.summary: Supports research and innovation to prevent, detect and deter crime, terrorism and radicalisation while safeguarding fundamental rights. Focus areas include improved investigative tools and methods, better risk and threat understanding, countering misuse of emerging technologies, and strengthening community resilience against polarisation, hate speech and gender-based violence. Projects are expected to deliver deployable capabilities for security practitioners, evidence for policy and operational practice, and stronger cooperation across law-enforcement, academia and industry.

Destination - Effective management of EU.summary: Targets more effective, humane and rights-compliant management of the EU’s external borders. It funds innovation for surveillance and situational awareness, secure and trusted travel facilitation, improved detection of illicit goods and threats in cargo, and more reliable identity and age-assessment approaches. Outcomes should enhance interoperability and operational usability for border authorities, improve efficiency for legitimate travellers and trade, and reinforce EU values and data protection.

Destination - Resilient Infrastructure.summary: Strengthens the resilience of critical entities and infrastructure against natural hazards, accidents, cyber and hybrid threats. Priorities include stress-testing tools, risk and vulnerability assessment, protective measures for complex systems, and continuity planning for essential services during crises. Expected results include better methodologies and decision-support for operators and public authorities, scalable solutions for diverse sectors, and improved preparedness for cascading and cross-border disruptions.

Destination - Disaster-Resilient Society.summary: Improves Europe’s capacity to anticipate, prepare for and respond to disasters, including climate-driven extremes and cascading events. Topics typically address multi-hazard risk modelling, early warning and situational awareness, new approaches to public risk communication and preparedness, and advanced tools and equipment for first responders, search and rescue and crisis management. Projects should strengthen civil protection capabilities, accelerate uptake of innovations and enhance societal resilience, including through citizen engagement and training.

Destination - Strengthened Security Rese.summary: Acts as a cross-cutting enabler for the whole cluster by reinforcing the security R&I ecosystem and accelerating uptake of innovative solutions. It supports disruptive innovation pathways (notably for SMEs), demand-led innovation and public procurement approaches, standardisation and interoperability work, and stronger collaboration between practitioners, innovators and policy makers. The destination aims to shorten time-to-deployment, improve market uptake of security technologies and ensure solutions are trustworthy, usable and aligned with EU legal and ethical requirements.
